extraction.one_line_summary: GESINK Robert led Jumbo-Visma to TTT victory by 13 seconds over Ineos, claiming his first grand tour leader's jersey at age 36.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma deliberately sent GESINK Robert across line first for ceremonial leader's jersey despite not being GC leader.
BikeExchange sacrificed riders early to maintain pace, losing over 41 seconds, revealing insufficient TTT depth for stage hunting.
Quick-Step climber-heavy squad finished 1 second behind Ineos, suggesting inexperience or poor pacing cost podium in short TTT.
Groupama-FDJ unexpectedly fast with 41-second margin over EF, indicating hidden TTT strength despite not bringing pure TT specialists.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: brought exceptionally strong TTT squad (Hamilton, Hepburn, Craddock) suggesting serious GC ambition beyond climbing strength
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: positioned in gold helmet with domestiques controlling pace, team prioritized his GC defense over stage result
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Quick-Step rode well but 1-second loss to Ineos suggests either pacing error or squad limitation in short TTT
rider_signals.GESINK Robert: positive: crossing line first at 36 years old on home roads ceremonial, but reveals Jumbo depth allows GC domestique showcase
